data.title: NatWest: Buy Rating Reiterated as Strong Guidance and Returns Support Unchanged 770 GBp Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Perlie Mong of Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating on NatWest Group, retaining the price target of p770.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Perlie Mong has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a solid first-quarter performance and improved forward guidance. While profit before tax excluding one-offs was slightly below market expectations because of softer non-interest income, net interest income, costs, and impairments were largely as anticipated, and loan growth of about 2% underscores healthy business momentum.

He also highlights that management has raised full-year income guidance to the top end of the prior range, supported by stronger volumes and a more favorable interest-rate outlook with no cuts now expected. In Mong’s view, NatWest can sustain a return on tangible equity near 20%, offers an attractive total yield above 10% in 2027–2028, and still trades on modest valuation multiples of roughly 1.5x 2026 tangible book value and 7x 2027 earnings, justifying a reiterated Buy with an unchanged 770 GBp price target.

In another report released on April 16, UBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a p780.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Steed Maintains Hold on Balanced Risk-Reward, Cutting Price Target to $80 Despite Solid Q1 Beat and Slight Guidance Raise
data.description:
Analyst Travis Steed from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Hold rating on Merit Medical Systems and decreased the price target to $80.00 from $100.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Travis Steed has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a solid first‑quarter beat on revenue, earnings, and margins, along with early evidence that the new segment structure is supporting mid‑single to high‑single‑digit growth across the portfolio. Management also raised full‑year revenue guidance slightly and reiterated the EPS outlook, signaling confidence but maintaining a cautious stance as they typically avoid major guidance changes this early in the year.

At the same time, Steed sees reasons for restraint, such as the modest organic growth outlook, the headwind from the DualCap divestiture, and EPS dilution associated with the View Point acquisition, which together temper the near‑term upside. While he views the View Point deal as strategically attractive for expanding the breast localization addressable market and leveraging the existing sales force, he adjusted his model and cut the price target from $100 to $80, leaving the risk‑reward profile balanced rather than compelling at current levels.

Steed covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, Stryker, and Medtronic. According to TipRanks, Steed has an average return of 0.2% and a 47.34% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $74.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
